articles.title: 👋 GOODBYE LOW TEST COVERAGE AND SLOW QA CYCLES
articles.summary: Bugs sneak out when less than 80% of user flows are tested before shipping. However, getting that kind of coverage (and staying there) is hard and pricey for any team.

articles.title: Schedule a demo to learn more
5 YEARS AND $5M LATER: INVENTING A NEW PROGRAMMING LANGUAGE FOR WEB DEVELOPMENT WAS A MISTAKE
articles.summary: The co-founder of Wasp reflects on the decision to build a custom programming language for web development, concluding that it was a mistake due to huge friction with developer adoption and the high cost of maintaining custom IDE tooling. As a result, the framework is transitioning to a TypeScript-based SDK to provide a more familiar interface.

articles.title: HOW WE MADE WINDOW JOIN PARALLEL AND VECTORIZED
articles.summary: QuestDB recently optimized its WINDOW JOIN operator by implementing data-level parallelism and SIMD-vectorized aggregation kernels, resulting in performance gains up to 25x faster than competitors. By using a dedicated operator that recognizes window structures and organizes data into contiguous memory buffers, the system minimizes overhead and maximizes CPU efficiency for demanding time-series workloads.

articles.title: TOKENMAXXING, PROMOMAXXING, AND MISALIGNED INCENTIVES IN TECH
articles.summary: Performance metrics like AI token usage or project complexity can become "perverse incentives" that encourage engineers to prioritize visible activity over actual productivity. Examples like Goodhart's Law and the Cobra Effect show that companies must carefully align incentive structures to focus on meaningful outcomes rather than easily gamed inputs.

articles.title: THE EMACSIFICATION OF SOFTWARE
articles.summary: The Emacsification of software is where AI agents allow developers to easily create bespoke, native user interfaces to solve personal productivity "itches." The future of software lies in highly configurable, personal tools rather than generic, one-size-fits-all applications.

articles.title: CUT YOUR AGENT'S TOOL CALLS BY 40%
articles.summary: Airbyte Agents [11] pre-indexes your business data into a unified, search-optimized layer so your agents stop paginating, deduplicating, and burning tokens at runtime. Expect 40% fewer tool calls and up to 80% fewer tokens vs. raw APIs and third-party MCPs. Deploy via SDK or

articles.title: CODEBUFF
articles.summary: Codebuff is an open-source AI coding assistant that uses a multi-agent system to plan, edit, and validate complex codebase changes via natural language. It supports any model on OpenRouter and has both a CLI for local development and an SDK for integrating custom agent workflows.

articles.title: REACT DOCTOR
articles.summary: React Doctor is an open-source tool that scans codebases to provide a health score and actionable diagnostics for catching poor React patterns, specifically designed to help coding agents follow best practices. It integrates with frameworks like Next.js and Vite.

articles.title: A HISTORY OF IDES AT GOOGLE
articles.summary: The evolution of development environments at Google has been from a fragmented landscape of individual choices to the widespread adoption of Cider V, a cloud-based IDE leveraging a VSCode frontend. It shows how internal tooling at scale requires balancing developer preference with the massive productivity gains found in a unified, extensible platform integrated with company-specific systems.

articles.title: MALWARE CREW TEAMPCP OPEN-SOURCES ITS SHAI-HULUD WORM ON GITHUB
articles.summary: The malware group TeamPCP has reportedly open-sourced its Shai-Hulud worm on GitHub under an MIT License, allowing anyone to modify and spread the credential-stealing code. Security researchers have already seen multiple forks of the repository.

articles.title: WHAT ARE THE _MOST_ CONFIDENT TEAMS USING TO BUILD AI IN PRODUCTION?
articles.summary: Inngest's recent survey of 130 engineers showed only 19% are very confident in their app's ability to scale. See their stack [17].

articles.title: GOOGLEBOOK
articles.summary: Google has released its new Googlebook pre-order site, designed for natural Gemini and AI usage.

articles.title: WHY I'M LEAVING GITHUB FOR FORGEJO
articles.summary: This dev transitioned from GitHub to a self-hosted Forgejo instance due to concerns over Microsoft's AI-driven direction, lack of independent leadership, and US jurisdictional data risks.

articles.title: HOW I MOVED MY DIGITAL STACK TO EUROPE
articles.summary: The article details a comprehensive migration from US-based SaaS platforms to a European-hosted digital stack to achieve greater digital sovereignty, data privacy, and infrastructural autonomy.

articles.title: IS THIS WHY SCIENCE ADVANCES ONE FUNERAL AT A TIME?
articles.summary: As researchers age, they tend to shift from producing disruptive, field-changing innovations to focusing on "connective novelty," which involves linking existing ideas rather than challenging established paradigms.
